q: What is the _rescued_data column in Auto Loader, exactly what lands there, and how do you parse it downstream?
a: _rescued_data is a STRING column Auto Loader automatically appends to every bronze table it writes. A row's unmatched data lands there as a JSON blob in three situations: the source has a column that is not in the current inferred schema (new field); the column exists in the schema but the value cannot be cast to the declared type; or the column name differs only in casing from the schema's declared name (case mismatch). The blob always includes the rescued column values keyed by their original source names, plus a special _filepath key containing the source file path for traceability. In rescue evolution mode, this column is the only signal of schema drift — no stream restart, no alert. To act on rescued data downstream you parse it with from_json and then either promote specific keys into first-class columns in silver, route rows with non-null _rescued_data to a quarantine table, or alert when the rescue rate rises above a threshold. The mistake is writing downstream queries that ignore _rescued_data — you silently lose all drifted fields.
fj: I'd check _rescued_data occasionally but it's mostly empty so I don't worry about it.
fs: _rescued_data is only empty when the schema is stable. In rescue mode it fills silently on schema drift — no failure, no alert. If you don't monitor it and parse rescued fields into silver, you're losing data without knowing it. Wire a quality check on COUNT(*) WHERE _rescued_data IS NOT NULL as a pipeline alert.

code.label: Parse _rescued_data and promote new column
lines: from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, StringType

code.label: Monitor rescue rate — alert if nonzero
lines: COUNT_IF(_rescued_data IS NOT NULL) AS rescued_rows,
